overall.assessment: Podcast o. Kennyja Yanga predstavlja iznimno vjernu artikulaciju katoličke vizije obrazovanja, naglašavajući da ono nije puko stjecanje informacija, već cjelovita transformacija osobe usmjerena prema njezinu konačnom cilju. Njegov fokus na relacijsku narav ljudske osobe, utemeljenu na trinitarnoj teologiji i Akvinčevoj misli, izravno korelira s crkvenim naukom o dostojanstvu pojedinca i važnosti osobnog susreta. Evaluacija AI-ja kao alata koji ne smije zamijeniti ljudsku prosudbu i 'srce' u potpunosti je usklađena s najnovijim papinskim smjernicama o tehnološkom napretku.
seeds_of_logos: Obrazovanje kao 'osobna transformacija' (metanoia) umjesto puke akumulacije informacija.
Relacijska ontologija: čovjek je definiran odnosima, što odražava trinitarnu sliku Boga.
Povratak liberalnim umjetnostima i triviumu kao putu prema autentičnoj ljudskoj slobodi.
Obitelj kao primarna i nezamjenjiva škola vrlina i ljudskosti.
Razlikovanje ljudske inteligencije (utjelovljene i srčane) od algoritamske obrade podataka.
Sokratovska metoda i kritičko vrednovanje kao obrana od fragmentacije pažnje i individualizma.
concerns: Rizik od 'uredske teologije' i gubitka zanata (know-how) zbog pretjeranog oslanjanja na automatizaciju.
Individualizam i pesimizam mladih koji proizlaze iz digitalne izolacije i gubitka smisla.
Mogućnost da AI ograniči ljudski pogled na svijet samo na ono što je mjerljivo brojevima.
overall.theological_context: Katolički nauk definira obrazovanje kao proces koji vodi autentičnom ljudskom razvoju i vječnoj sreći s Bogom. Papa Franjo i papa Leon XIV. naglašavaju da AI mora ostati pod ljudskom kontrolom kako bi se zaštitilo dostojanstvo osobe, jer samo ljudsko 'srce' može otkriti smisao postojanja. Dokument 'Educating Today and Tomorrow' potvrđuje da je cilj obrazovanja formacija ljudi sposobnih za kritičko mišljenje i služenje općem dobru.

citations.document_title: Meeting with the university community in the Atma Jaya Catholic University of Jakarta (October 12, 1989) - Speech (Pope John Paul II),
Message of the Holy Father for the Paris Summit on AI [10-11 February 2025] (7 February 2025) (Pope Francis),
Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)
Educating Today and Tomorrow: A Renewing Passion

score_breakdown.theme: Obrazovanje, tehnologija i ljudski element u svijetu koji se mijenja
score_breakdown.theme_en: Education, technology and the human element in a world that is changing

iterations.theme: Obrazovanje, tehnologija i ljudski element u svijetu koji se mijenja

sections.subtitle: Katolička vizija obrazovanja: Od drevnih korijena do suvremenih izazova
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang objašnjava povijesne korijene katoličkog obrazovanja.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett započinje razgovor s ocem Kennyjem Yangom o katoličkoj viziji obrazovanja u kontekstu nadolazećih tehnologija. Otac Kenny ističe da se katolički model obrazovanja razlikuje od svjetovnog, podsjećajući na osnivanje prvih sveučilišta poput Bologne (1088.), Oxforda, Pariza i Salamance, koja su proizašla iz katedralnih škola. U to vrijeme, ideja sveučilišta bila je usredotočena na **liberalne umjetnosti** – trivijum (gramatika, logika, retorika) i kvadrivijum (znanost, matematika, glazba, aritmetika, astronomija). Ove su znanosti imale za cilj 'osloboditi' čovjeka, omogućujući mu da 'čini dobro', pri čemu sloboda nije shvaćena kao autonomija, već kao sposobnost djelovanja u skladu s dobrom.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak točno prikazuje povijesni razvoj katoličkog obrazovanja iz katedralnih škola u prva sveučilišta te naglašava važnost liberalnih umjetnosti.
magisterium.enrichment: Katoličko obrazovanje tradicionalno koristi trivij (gramatika, retorika, dijalektika) kao temelj za razumijevanje najviših načela i uzroka stvarnosti. Svrha ovog modela nije samo prijenos informacija, već osposobljavanje učenika za pronalaženje smisla i kontemplaciju.

citations.document_title: Scholarship and Sanctity: A Lesson Aquinas Teaches the Priest and Seminarian
Catholic Encyclopedia, The Faculty of Arts
Communio: International Catholic Review - Summer, 1992

magisterium.assessment_en: The passage accurately depicts the historical development of Catholic education from cathedral schools into the first universities and emphasizes the importance of the liberal arts.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Catholic education traditionally uses the trivium (grammar, rhetoric, dialectic) as a foundation for understanding the highest principles and causes of reality. The purpose of this model is not only the transmission of information, but the training of students for finding meaning and contemplation.

sections.subtitle_en: Catholic vision of education: From ancient roots to contemporary challenges
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang explains the historical roots of Catholic education.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett begins a conversation with Father Kenny Yang about the Catholic vision of education in the context of upcoming technologies. Father Kenny emphasizes that the Catholic model of education differs from the secular one, recalling the founding of the first universities such as Bologna (1088), Oxford, Pariz, and Salamanca, which emerged from cathedral schools. At that time, the idea of the university was focused on **liberal arts** – the trivium (grammar, logic, rhetoric) and the quadrivium (science, mathematics, music, arithmetic, astronomy). These sciences aimed to 'liberate' man, enabling him to 'do good', whereby freedom was not understood as autonomy, but as the ability to act in accordance with the good.

sections.subtitle: Transformacija obrazovanja: Od oslobađanja do stjecanja 'papira'
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang govori o promjeni fokusa obrazovanja na stjecanje diplome.
sections.content: Otac Kenny Yang primjećuje značajnu promjenu u percepciji obrazovanja od srednjeg vijeka do 20. stoljeća. Dok su nekada liberalne umjetnosti imale za cilj oslobađanje pojedinca, danas mnogi studenti upisuju fakultete s primarnim ciljem stjecanja diplome ('papira') kako bi osigurali posao i zaradu. Obrazovanje se tako prebacilo na učenje praktičnih vještina i zanata, gubeći svoju izvornu svrhu pružanja istinske slobode kroz duboko razumijevanje i formaciju.

magisterium.assessment: Analiza je usklađena s crkvenom kritikom svođenja obrazovanja na puku tehničku obuku ili stjecanje vještina za tržište rada.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva upozorava da obrazovanje ne smije biti samo 'učenje kako raditi', već inicijacija u duhovnu baštinu zajednice. Istinsko obrazovanje oslobađa osobu za odgovorno korištenje slobode i potragu za istinom, nadilazeći čisto materijalističke ciljeve.

citations.document_title: Communio: International Catholic Review - Summer, 1992
The Three Pillars of Catholic Education
To a group of Bishops from the United States of America on their ad Limina visit (October 28, 1983) - Speech

magisterium.assessment_en: The analysis is aligned with the church's criticism of reducing education to mere technical training or the acquisition of skills for the labor market.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church warns that education must not be only 'learning how to work', but an initiation into the spiritual heritage of the community. True education frees the person for the responsible use of freedom and the search for truth, transcending purely materialistic goals.

sections.subtitle_en: Transformation of education: From liberation to the acquisition of 'paper'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang speaks about the change of focus of education to obtaining a degree.
sections.content_en: Father Kenny Yang notices a significant change in the perception of education from the Middle Ages to the 20th century. While once the liberal arts had as a goal the liberation of the individual, today many students enroll in faculties with the primary goal of obtaining a diploma ('paper') in order to secure a job and earnings. Education has thus shifted to the learning of practical skills and crafts, losing its original purpose of providing true freedom through deep understanding and formation.

sections.subtitle: Odnosi kao srž obrazovanja: Lekcije iz trinitarne teologije i osobnog iskustva
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang objašnjava važnost odnosa u obrazovanju kroz prizmu trinitarne teologije.
sections.content: U potrazi za povratkom izvornom smislu obrazovanja, otac Kenny Yang predlaže učenje iz **trinitarne teologije**. Pozivajući se na svetog Tomu Akvinskog, objašnjava da je božanska osoba definirana odnosima – Otac je očinstvo, Sin je odnos pripadnosti Ocu, a Duh Sveti je dah Oca i Sina. Slično tome, u obrazovanju su odnosi ključni. Otac Kenny dijeli osobno iskustvo s profesorom Joséom Maríom Pardom u Pamploni, koji je naglašavao da studenti dolaze na predavanja ne samo zbog sadržaja koji mogu pronaći drugdje, već zbog interakcije i dodane vrijednosti koju profesor pruža. Benjamin Crockett se nadovezuje na to, ističući svoje iskustvo na Sveučilištu u Kaliforniji u Berkeleyju, gdje su najtransformirajući aspekti njegovog obrazovanja bili izvan predavanja, kroz prijateljstva i odnose s profesorima i mentorima. Obojica se slažu da je zajedničko iskustvo, posebno u formativnim godinama, nezamjenjivo za osobni rast i otkrivanje identiteta.

magisterium.assessment: Prijedlog o. Kennyja o trinitarnoj teologiji kao modelu za odnose u obrazovanju duboko je ukorijenjen u tomističkoj misli.
magisterium.enrichment: Sveti Toma Akvinski definira božanske osobe kroz njihove međusobne odnose unutar jedinstva božanske naravi. U obrazovnom kontekstu, škole su pozvane nuditi prostor za razvoj povjerljivih odnosa koje digitalna sfera sama po sebi ne može pružiti.

citations.document_title: Commentary on Sentences
Commentary on Sentences
Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)

magisterium.assessment_en: Fr. Kenny's proposal on Trinitarian theology as a model for relationships in education is deeply rooted in Thomistic thought.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Saint Thomas Aquinas defines divine persons through their mutual relations within the unity of the divine nature. In an educational context, schools are called to offer space for the development of trusting relations which the digital sphere by itself cannot provide.

sections.subtitle_en: Relationships as the Core of Education: Lessons from Trinitarian Theology and Personal Experience
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang explains the importance of relationships in education through the prism of Trinitarian theology.
sections.content_en: In the search for a return to the original meaning of education, Father Kenny Yang proposes learning from **Trinitarian theology**. Referring to Saint Thomas Aquinas, he explains that a divine person is defined by relations – the Father is fatherhood, the Son is the relation of belonging to the Father, and the Holy Spirit is the breath of the Father and the Son. Similarly, in education, relations are key. Father Kenny shares a personal experience with professor José María Pardo in Pamplona, who emphasized that students come to lectures not only because of the content they can find elsewhere, but because of the interaction and added value that the professor provides. Benjamin Crockett builds on that, highlighting his experience at the University of California in Berkeley, where the most transforming aspects of his education were outside of lectures, through friendships and relations with professors and mentors. Both agree that a shared experience, especially in the formative years, is irreplaceable for personal growth and the discovery of identity.

sections.subtitle: Izazovi modernog doba: Izolacija, nedostatak pažnje i uloga obitelji
sections.screenshot_description: Benjamin Crockett i Otac Kenny Yang razgovaraju o problemima izolacije i smanjene pažnje kod mladih.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett i otac Kenny Yang prepoznaju ozbiljne izazove modernog obrazovanja, uključujući **izolaciju** i **nedostatak pažnje**. Benjamin primjećuje da mlađe generacije imaju poteškoća s dugotrajnom koncentracijom, preferirajući kratke sadržaje umjesto, primjerice, trosatnih filmova. Korištenje više ekrana istovremeno (mobitel, računalo, TV) dodatno fragmentira pažnju. Otac Kenny naglašava da obitelj, kao 'prva škola', ima ključnu ulogu u prenošenju osnovnih životnih vještina i vrijednosti. Međutim, raspad obitelji i sve veća izolacija djece, koja provode više vremena sama kod kuće umjesto u parku, prebacuju dodatni teret na učitelje. Oni sada moraju rješavati mnogo više od samog podučavanja, uključujući probleme vezane uz pametne telefone i tjeskobu, što dodatno otežava fokusiranje studenata na materijal, čak i na poslijediplomskoj razini.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak ispravno identificira izazove digitalnog doba i potvrđuje ulogu obitelji kao primarne odgojne zajednice.
magisterium.enrichment: Obitelj je 'domaća Crkva' i prva škola ljudskih vrijednosti gdje se uči mudra uporaba slobode i kritički odmak od medijskih poruka. Suvremena hiperstimulacija digitalnih medija može dovesti do apatije i umora u traženju istine, što zahtijeva obnovljeni obrazovni savez.

citations.document_title: Christifideles Laici
Amoris Laetitia
Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)
Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)

magisterium.assessment_en: The passage correctly identifies the challenges of the digital age and confirms the role of the family as the primary educational community.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The family is the 'domestic Church' and the first school of human values where the wise use of freedom and a critical distance from media messages are learned. Contemporary hyperstimulation of digital media can lead to apathy and fatigue in the search for truth, which requires a renewed educational alliance.

sections.subtitle_en: Challenges of the modern age: Isolation, lack of attention and the role of the family
sections.screenshot_description_en: Benjamin Crockett and Father Kenny Yang talk about the problems of isolation and reduced attention among the youth.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett and Father Kenny Yang recognize serious challenges of modern education, including **isolation** and **lack of attention**. Benjamin notices that younger generations have difficulties with long-term concentration, preferring short content instead of, for example, three-hour movies. The use of multiple screens simultaneously (mobile phone, computer, TV) further fragments attention. Father Kenny emphasizes that the family, as the 'first school', has a key role in transmitting basic life skills and values. However, the breakdown of the family and the increasing isolation of children, who spend more time alone at home instead of in the park, shift an additional burden onto teachers. They now must resolve much more than just teaching, including problems related to smartphones and anxiety, which further makes it difficult for students to focus on the material, even at the postgraduate level.

sections.subtitle: Vrijednost osobnog susreta: Kritika 'uredske teologije'
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang objašnjava koncept 'uredske teologije' i važnost osobnog kontakta.
sections.content: Iskustvo pandemije COVID-19, iako teško, otkrilo je duboku ljudsku potrebu za osobnim susretima. Otac Kenny Yang ističe da, unatoč početnom entuzijazmu za online nastavu i webinare, ljudi su shvatili da im nedostaje izravan kontakt. U teologiji postoji izraz 'teologia fatta a tavolino' (uredska teologija), koji opisuje izolirano, teorijsko razmišljanje bez konteksta i dijaloga. Prava teologija, objašnjava otac Kenny, uvijek se razvijala u zajednici, kroz razgovor, javne rasprave i odgovaranje na prigovore drugih. To naglašava da je učenje i razmišljanje inherentno zajedničko, a ne individualno iskustvo.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak izvrsno promiče katolički nauk o teologiji kao dijalogu koji izvire iz osobnog susreta, a ne iz izolirane teorije.
magisterium.enrichment: Papa Franjo i Leo XIV. naglašavaju da teologija mora biti 'gostoljubiva' i utjelovljena u konkretnim ljudskim okolnostima, izbjegavajući krute sheme. Autentična teologija je 'teologija u hodu' koja se hrani molitvom i susretom s Kristom u drugima.

citations.document_title: Meeting on the theme "Theology after Veritatis Gaudium in the context of the Mediterranean" (Pope Francis),
To Participants in the Seminar promoted by the Pontifical Academy of Theology [11–12 September 2025]

magisterium.assessment_en: The passage excellently promotes Catholic teaching on theology as a dialogue that springs from a personal encounter, and not from an isolated theory.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Pope Francis and Leo XIV emphasize that theology must be 'hospitable' and embodied in concrete human circumstances, avoiding rigid schemes. Authentic theology is 'theology on the move' which feeds on prayer and the encounter with Christ in others.

sections.subtitle_en: The value of personal encounter: Critique of 'office theology'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang explains the concept of 'office theology' and the importance of personal contact.
sections.content_en: The experience of the COVID-19 pandemic, although difficult, revealed a deep human need for personal encounters. Father Kenny Yang points out that, despite the initial enthusiasm for online classes and webinars, people realized that they lacked direct contact. In theology, there is the expression 'teologia fatta a tavolino' (office theology), which describes isolated, theoretical thinking without context and dialogue. True theology, Father Kenny explains, always developed in community, through conversation, public debates, and responding to the objections of others. This emphasizes that learning and thinking is an inherently communal, rather than an individual experience.

sections.subtitle: Individualizam i pesimizam studenata u eri AI-ja
sections.screenshot_description: Benjamin Crockett govori o nedostatku optimizma kod današnjih studenata.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett primjećuje da je njegovo vlastito obrazovanje bilo izrazito individualističko, fokusirano na dobre ocjene i izvannastavne aktivnosti, s ciljem upisa na dobar fakultet i osiguranja posla. Iako priznaje vrijednost zdrave konkurencije, ističe da je nedostajao osjećaj zajedništva. Danas, razgovarajući s obitelji i prijateljima čija djeca pohađaju srednju školu, Benjamin primjećuje značajan nedostatak optimizma. Studenti se suočavaju s 'obrazovanjem za preživljavanje', gdje je cilj samo 'probijanje' kroz sustav, često uz osjećaj da će umjetna inteligencija preuzeti poslove. Ovaj pesimizam, koji otac Kenny Yang također prepoznaje, stvara zabrinutost za budućnost mladih generacija.

magisterium.assessment: Sadržaj je usklađen s crkvenom brigom za cjeloviti razvoj osobe nasuprot pukom individualizmu i pesimizmu mladih.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva prepoznaje da su mladi danas posebno ranjivi na manipulaciju algoritmima koji mogu utjecati na njihovu nadu i slobodu. Obrazovanje mora poticati 'ljudsku originalnost' i zajedništvo kako bi se nadvladao osjećaj izolacije u digitalnom dobu.

citations.document_title: To the Participants in the meeting organised by the Foundation for the Study and Research on Childhood and Adolescence (13 November 2025)
Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)

magisterium.assessment_en: The content is aligned with the Church's concern for the integral development of the person as opposed to mere individualism and the pessimism of the youth.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church recognizes that young people today are especially vulnerable to manipulation by algorithms which can affect their hope and freedom. Education must promote 'human originality' and communion in order to overcome the feeling of isolation in the digital age.

sections.subtitle_en: Individualism and pessimism of students in the era of AI
sections.screenshot_description_en: Benjamin Crockett speaks about the lack of optimism among today's students.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett notices that his own education was distinctly individualistic, focused on good grades and extracurricular activities, with the goal of enrolling in a good college and securing a job. Although he acknowledges the value of healthy competition, he points out that a sense of community was missing. Today, talking with family and friends whose children attend high school, Benjamin notices a significant lack of optimism. Students face 'education for survival', where the goal is only 'breaking through' the system, often with the feeling that artificial intelligence will take over jobs. This pessimism, which Father Kenny Yang also recognizes, creates concern for the future of young generations.

sections.subtitle: Preispitivanje ocjenjivanja: Obrazovanje kao putovanje osobne transformacije
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang objašnjava svoju viziju obrazovanja kao putovanja osobne transformacije.
sections.content: Otac Kenny Yang kritizira tradicionalni sustav ocjenjivanja, sugerirajući da on često ne služi svrsi istinskog učenja. Spominje članak o 'neocjenjivanju' gdje studenti na početku tečaja sami određuju željenu ocjenu, a profesor im pomaže da je postignu. Za oca Kennyja, cilj obrazovanja je putovanje od točke A do točke B, pri čemu je ključna **osobna transformacija**. Kao primjer navodi svoj tečaj o Trojstvu, gdje je cilj pomoći studentima da bolje upoznaju Trojstvo ne samo konceptualno, već i da to znanje promijeni njihov život, molitvu i društvene odnose. Ocjenjivanje bi se, prema njemu, trebalo fokusirati na napredak svakog pojedinca u tom smjeru, što zahtijeva individualni pristup svakom studentu.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak snažno promiče katoličko viđenje obrazovanja kao puta osobne transformacije i sazrijevanja u vjeri i ljudskosti.
magisterium.enrichment: Cilj katoličkog obrazovanja nije samo stjecanje informacija, već 'unutarnja obnova' i osposobljavanje za odgovor na Božji poziv. Istinsko učenje uključuje otvorenost prema istini i dobru, što vodi do preobrazbe cijelog bića, a ne samo do akademskog uspjeha.

citations.document_title: Gravissimum Educationis
Gravissimum Educationis

magisterium.assessment_en: The passage strongly promotes the Catholic vision of education as a path of personal transformation and maturation in faith and humanity.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The goal of Catholic education is not only the acquisition of information, but 'inner renewal' and training for the response to God's call. True learning includes openness toward truth and good, which leads to the transformation of the whole being, and not only to academic success.

sections.subtitle_en: Rethinking grading: Education as a journey of personal transformation
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang explains his vision of education as a journey of personal transformation.
sections.content_en: Father Kenny Yang criticizes the traditional grading system, suggesting that it often does not serve the purpose of true learning. He mentions an article about 'ungrading' where students at the beginning of the course determine their desired grade themselves, and the professor helps them to achieve it. For Father Kenny, the goal of education is a journey from point A to point B, in which **personal transformation** is key. As an example, he cites his course on the Trinity, where the goal is to help students to know the Trinity better not only conceptually, but also that this knowledge changes their life, prayer, and social relationships. Grading should, according to him, focus on the progress of each individual in that direction, which requires an individual approach to each student.

sections.subtitle: AI kao alat za nastavnike i izazov autentičnosti studentskog rada
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang govori o korištenju AI-ja u nastavi i izazovima autentičnosti.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett izražava nadu da će umjetna inteligencija (AI) osloboditi nastavnike administrativnih zadataka, omogućujući im da posvete više vremena studentima. Otac Kenny Yang potvrđuje da AI može biti koristan alat u tom smjeru. Međutim, postavlja se pitanje **autentičnosti studentskog rada**, posebno s pojavom alata poput ChatGBT-a. Tradicionalni eseji, nekada standardni zadatak, sada se mogu generirati AI-jem. Otac Kenny objašnjava da on i njegovi kolege na fakultetu ne žele podučavati studente da rade ono što AI može bolje. Stoga je ove godine prestao dodjeljivati pisane radove, umjesto toga se fokusirajući na **usmene ispite** i **tutorijalne skupine** koje potiču raspravu i osobni angažman. Ipak, naglašava da pisanje ostaje ključno za razmišljanje, te potiče studente da i dalje pišu, ali na način koji potiče dublje promišljanje, a ne samo generiranje sadržaja.

magisterium.assessment: Usklađeno s magisterijem koji AI vidi kao alat koji mora služiti ljudskom dostojanstvu i autentičnosti rada.
magisterium.enrichment: Papa Leo XIV. upozorava da pristup podacima ne smije biti zamjena za mudrost i kritičko razmišljanje. Potrebno je osigurati da AI ostane saveznik u obrazovanju, štiteći sposobnost učenika za čuđenje, kontemplaciju i autentično stvaralaštvo.

citations.document_title: Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)
To Participants in the Conference "Artificial Intelligence and Care for Our Common Home" (5 December 2025)

magisterium.assessment_en: Aligned with the magisterium which sees AI as a tool that must serve human dignity and the authenticity of work.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Pope Leo XIV warns that access to data must not be a substitute for wisdom and critical thinking. It is necessary to ensure that AI remains an ally in education, protecting the students' ability for wonder, contemplation, and authentic creativity.

sections.subtitle_en: AI as a tool for teachers and the challenge of the authenticity of student work
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang speaks about the use of AI in teaching and the challenges of authenticity.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett expresses hope that artificial intelligence (AI) will free teachers from administrative tasks, allowing them to devote more time to students. Father Kenny Yang confirms that AI can be a useful tool in that direction. However, the question of the **authenticity of student work** arises, especially with the emergence of tools like ChatGBT. Traditional essays, once a standard task, can now be generated by AI. Father Kenny explains that he and his colleagues at the faculty do not want to teach students to do what AI can do better. Therefore, this year he stopped assigning written works, instead focusing on **oral exams** and **tutorial groups** that encourage discussion and personal engagement. Nevertheless, he emphasizes that writing remains crucial for thinking, and encourages students to continue writing, but in a way that encourages deeper reflection, and not just the generation of content.

sections.subtitle: Utjelovljenost i relacijski aspekt: Ključne razlike između ljudske i umjetne inteligencije
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang objašnjava razliku između ljudske i umjetne inteligencije.
sections.content: Na pitanje Benjamina Crocketta o temeljnoj razlici između ljudske i umjetne inteligencije, otac Kenny Yang ističe ključnu riječ: **utjelovljenost**. Ljudska inteligencija je utjelovljena jer smo mi jedinstvo tijela i duše. Naše znanje proizlazi iz osjetilnog iskustva – vidimo, čujemo, dodirujemo, a zatim apstrahiramo koncepte koji nam pomažu razumjeti stvarnost. AI, s druge strane, nije utjelovljena; ona nikada ne doživljava, ne dodiruje, ne kija. Stoga je ljudska inteligencija i **relacijska**, stječući znanje kroz odnose sa stvarima i ljudima. Otac Kenny navodi primjer samovozećih automobila: iako su napredni, još uvijek nisu sposobni nositi se s nepredvidivim situacijama na način na koji to može ljudski vozač, upravo zbog nedostatka utjelovljenog i relacijskog iskustva.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak izvrsno artikulira katolički nauk o jedinstvu tijela i duše te nužnosti utjelovljenog iskustva za ljudsku spoznaju.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva naglašava da ljudska inteligencija, za razliku od AI, posjeduje savjest i slobodu koje proizlaze iz njezine duhovne i tjelesne naravi. Autentična spoznaja uključuje osjetilnu percepciju koja se ne može svesti na puku digitalnu obradu podataka.

citations.document_title: Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)
Quo Vadis, Humanitas? Pensare L'Antropologia Cristiana di Fronte ad Alcuni Scenari sul Futuro Dell'Umano

magisterium.assessment_en: The passage excellently articulates Catholic teaching on the unity of body and soul and the necessity of embodied experience for human knowledge.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church emphasizes that human intelligence, unlike AI, possesses conscience and freedom which arise from its spiritual and bodily nature. Authentic knowledge includes sensory perception which cannot be reduced to mere digital data processing.

sections.subtitle_en: Embodiment and the relational aspect: Key differences between human and artificial intelligence
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang explains the difference between human and artificial intelligence.
sections.content_en: To the question of Benjamin Crockett about the fundamental difference between human and artificial intelligence, Father Kenny Yang highlights the key word: **embodiment**. Human intelligence is embodied because we are a unity of body and soul. Our knowledge arises from sensory experience – we see, hear, touch, and then we abstract concepts that help us understand reality. AI, on the other hand, is not embodied; it never experiences, does not touch, does not sneeze. Therefore, human intelligence is also **relational**, acquiring knowledge through relationships with things and people. Father Kenny cites the example of self-driving cars: although they are advanced, they are still not capable of dealing with unpredictable situations in the way a human driver can, precisely because of the lack of embodied and relational experience.

sections.subtitle: Gubitak 'zanata' i ljudskosti: Cijena prekomjernog oslanjanja na AI
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang govori o gubitku 'zanata' razmišljanja zbog prekomjernog oslanjanja na AI.
sections.content: Otac Kenny Yang upozorava na ono što gubimo prekomjernim oslanjanjem na alate umjetne inteligencije. Prvo, gubi se **'zanat' razmišljanja**. Korištenje AI-ja za pisanje e-pošte ili tekstova omogućuje nam da brzo dođemo od točke A do točke B, ali ne prolazimo kroz proces razmišljanja i 'utjelovljenog' iskustva. Uspoređuje to s vožnjom automobilom umjesto hodanja kako bi se 'odradili koraci' – cilj je postignut, ali iskustvo i proces su izostali. Stoga otac Kenny svjesno odvaja vrijeme za pisanje bez pomoći AI-ja, čak i s pogreškama, jer mu to pomaže u razmišljanju. Drugo, gubi se **ljudskost** ili ljudski karakter naših proizvoda. Savršeno napisani tekstovi generirani AI-jem, iako gramatički besprijekorni, gube autentičnost i osobni pečat koji proizlazi iz ljudskog razmišljanja, pa čak i pogrešaka.

magisterium.assessment: Usklađeno s upozorenjima Učiteljstva o riziku 'kognitivnog duga' i gubitka mentalne agilnosti zbog pretjerane delegacije razmišljanja strojevima.
magisterium.enrichment: Papa Leo XIV upozorava da brzina AI odgovora može ugasiti želju za postavljanjem pitanja, što je proces koji zahtijeva vrijeme i trud. Delegiranje razmišljanja i izračuna može smanjiti kreativnost i kritičku oštrinu pojedinca.

citations.document_title: Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)
Quo Vadis, Humanitas? Pensare L'Antropologia Cristiana di Fronte ad Alcuni Scenari sul Futuro Dell'Umano

magisterium.assessment_en: Aligned with the warnings of the Magisterium about the risk of 'cognitive debt' and the loss of mental agility due to the excessive delegation of thinking to machines.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Pope Leo XIV warns that the speed of AI responses can extinguish the desire for asking questions, which is a process that requires time and effort. Delegating thinking and calculations can reduce the creativity and critical sharpness of the individual.

sections.subtitle_en: Loss of 'craft' and humanity: The price of excessive reliance on AI
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang speaks about the loss of the 'craft' of thinking due to excessive reliance on AI.
sections.content_en: Father Kenny Yang warns about what we lose by excessive reliance on artificial intelligence tools. First, the **'craft' of thinking** is lost. Using AI for writing emails or texts allows us to quickly get from point A to point B, but we do not go through the process of thinking and 'embodied' experience. He compares this to driving a car instead of walking in order to 'do the steps' – the goal is achieved, but the experience and process are missing. Therefore, Father Kenny consciously sets aside time for writing without the help of AI, even with mistakes, because it helps him in thinking. Second, **humanity** or the human character of our products is lost. Perfectly written texts generated by AI, although grammatically flawless, lose the authenticity and personal touch that comes from human thinking, and even mistakes.

sections.subtitle: Kulturološke razlike u obrazovnim pristupima: Europa, SAD i Azija
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang objašnjava razlike u obrazovnim pristupima diljem svijeta.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett pita oca Kennyja o kulturološkim razlikama u obrazovanju koje primjećuje među svojim međunarodnim studentima. Otac Kenny Yang objašnjava da svaka geografska regija ima svoje specifičnosti. U **Europi**, studenti su tradicionalno navikli na **magisterijski stil predavanja**, gdje profesor drži izlaganje, a studenti slušaju. Sam naziv 'predavanje' (lat. *legere*) upućuje na čitanje. S druge strane, u **SAD-u**, obrazovanje je često **projektno orijentirano**, potičući kreativnost i poduzetnički duh kroz grupni rad i aktivno sudjelovanje. Otac Kenny primjećuje da je i u **Aziji** prisutan projektni pristup s naglaskom na studentsko sudjelovanje i grupni rad. Zaključuje da tradicionalni format predavanja više nije dovoljan u današnjem tehnološkom dobu, te da je dinamičniji pristup, koji uključuje aktivno sudjelovanje studenata, sve važniji.

magisterium.assessment: Opis pedagoških stilova je sociološki točan i u skladu s poštivanjem kulturnih različitosti u obrazovanju.
magisterium.enrichment: Katoličke obrazovne institucije pozvane su biti živa okruženja u kojima kršćanska vizija prožima svaku disciplinu, neovisno o specifičnom kulturnom stilu poučavanja. Cilj ostaje cjelovita formacija osobe usmjerena prema istini.

citations.document_title: To a Delegation of the Association of Catholic Colleges and Universities (3 June 2026)

magisterium.assessment_en: The description of pedagogical styles is sociologically accurate and in accordance with the respect for cultural differences in education.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Catholic educational institutions are called to be living environments in which the Christian vision permeates every discipline, regardless of the specific cultural style of teaching. The goal remains the integral formation of the person directed toward the truth.

sections.subtitle_en: Cultural differences in educational approaches: Europe, USA and Asia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang explains the differences in educational approaches throughout the world.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett asks Father Kenny about the cultural differences in education that he notices among his international students. Father Kenny Yang explains that every geographical region has its specificities. In **Europe**, students are traditionally accustomed to the **magisterial style of lecturing**, where the professor gives a presentation, and the students listen. The very name 'lecture' (lat. *legere*) refers to reading. On the other hand, in the **USA**, education is often **project-oriented**, encouraging creativity and entrepreneurial spirit through group work and active participation. Father Kenny notices that in **Asia** as well, a project approach is present with an emphasis on student participation and group work. He concludes that the traditional format of lecturing is no longer sufficient in today's technological age, and that a more dynamic approach, which includes the active participation of students, is increasingly important.

sections.subtitle: Priprema studenata za eru AI: Aktivno učenje i kritičko vrednovanje sadržaja
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang govori o pripremi studenata za eru umjetne inteligencije.
sections.content: Otac Kenny Yang, aktivno uključen u tehnološki prostor, objašnjava kako priprema svoje studente za eru umjetne inteligencije. Njegov glavni cilj je potaknuti ih da budu **aktivni učenici**, a ne pasivni konzumenti sadržaja generiranog AI-jem. Umjesto da jednostavno 'progutaju' ono što AI izbaci, studenti moraju aktivno sudjelovati u procesu učenja. To uključuje dodjeljivanje čitanja i pitanja prije nastave, što im pomaže da dođu u razred s već formiranim okvirom razmišljanja. Otac Kenny naglašava važnost 'zanata' razmišljanja, čitanja, probavljanja teksta i kontakta s primarnim izvorima, umjesto da se oslanjaju na AI za sažetke. Sveučilišta se također prilagođavaju, osnivajući radne skupine za AI i koristeći ga za istraživanje, ali s naglaskom na to da AI služi kao alat za pronalaženje izvora i poticanje razmišljanja, a ne za zamjenu samog procesa učenja.

magisterium.assessment: Promiče proaktivni pristup obrazovanju koji potiče kritičko razmišljanje i odgovorno korištenje tehnologije u skladu s papinskim smjernicama.
magisterium.enrichment: Ključno je da mladi razviju vještine kritičkog razmišljanja i pamćenja kako bi odgovorno oblikovali svijet, umjesto da postanu pasivni korisnici. Obrazovanje o AI mora uključivati i učenje o tome kada i u koju svrhu se ona ne bi trebala koristiti.

citations.document_title: To a Delegation of the Association of Catholic Colleges and Universities (3 June 2026)
Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)

magisterium.assessment_en: It promotes a proactive approach to education that encourages critical thinking and responsible use of technology in accordance with papal guidelines.
magisterium.enrichment_en: It is crucial that young people develop skills of critical thinking and memory in order to responsibly shape the world, instead of becoming passive users. Education about AI must also include learning about when and for what purpose it should not be used.

sections.subtitle_en: Preparation of students for the AI era: Active learning and critical evaluation of content
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang speaks about the preparation of students for the era of artificial intelligence.
sections.content_en: Father Kenny Yang, actively involved in the technological space, explains how he prepares his students for the era of artificial intelligence. His main goal is to encourage them to be **active learners**, and not passive consumers of content generated by AI. Instead of simply 'swallowing' what AI spits out, students must actively participate in the learning process. This includes assigning readings and questions before class, which helps them come to class with an already formed framework of thinking. Father Kenny emphasizes the importance of the 'craft' of thinking, reading, digesting text, and contact with primary sources, instead of relying on AI for summaries. Universities are also adapting, establishing working groups for AI and using it for research, but with an emphasis on AI serving as a tool for finding sources and stimulating thinking, and not for replacing the learning process itself.

sections.subtitle: Budućnost učionice: Povratak osobnim interakcijama i Sokratovoj metodi
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang predviđa budućnost učionice s naglaskom na osobne interakcije.
sections.content: Otac Kenny Yang predviđa da će se budućnost učionice vjerojatno vratiti **osobnim interakcijama**. Uspoređuje to s iskustvom COVID-19 pandemije, kada je početni entuzijazam za online nastavu ustupio mjesto spoznaji o vrijednosti izravnog kontakta. Slično tome, vjeruje da će 'mjehur' oko umjetne inteligencije s vremenom splasnuti, a ljudi će shvatiti da više cijene osobno razmišljanje, raspravljanje i suočavanje s idejama u neposrednom okruženju. Predviđa kraće, manje predavački orijentirane učionice, gdje će profesori djelovati kao **moderatori**, koristeći **Sokratovu metodu** kako bi pomogli studentima da sami dođu do odgovora, poput 'babice' koja pomaže pri rođenju ideja. Ovaj pristup, vjeruje, bit će korisniji u dobu AI-ja.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak snažno promiče katolički pedagoški ideal koji naglašava izravan i osoban kontakt između učitelja i učenika kao temelj obrazovanja.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva naučava da je osobni odnos dijalog, a ne monolog, te da je takva interakcija povlaštena prilika za svjedočenje vjere. Autentični ljudski odnosi i dijalog pripremaju put za otvaranje Istini koja je Krist.

citations.document_title: Lay Catholics in Schools: Witnesses to Faith
citations.document_author: The Sacred Congregation for Catholic Education

citations.document_title: Lay Catholics in Schools: Witnesses to Faith
citations.document_author: The Sacred Congregation for Catholic Education

magisterium.assessment_en: The passage strongly promotes the Catholic pedagogical ideal which emphasizes direct and personal contact between teacher and student as the foundation of education.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that a personal relationship is a dialogue, and not a monologue, and that such interaction is a privileged opportunity for witnessing the faith. Authentic human relationships and dialogue prepare the way for opening to the Truth which is Christ.

sections.subtitle_en: The future of the classroom: Return to personal interactions and the Socratic method
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang predicts the future of the classroom with an emphasis on personal interactions.
sections.content_en: Father Kenny Yang predicts that the future of the classroom will likely return to **personal interactions**. He compares this with the experience of the COVID-19 pandemic, when the initial enthusiasm for online classes gave way to the realization of the value of direct contact. Similarly, he believes that the 'bubble' around artificial intelligence will burst over time, and people will realize that they value personal thinking, discussion, and confronting ideas in an immediate environment more. He predicts shorter, less lecture-oriented classrooms, where professors will act as **moderators**, using the **Socratic method** to help students arrive at answers themselves, like a 'midwife' who helps with the birth of ideas. This approach, he believes, will be more useful in the age of AI.

sections.subtitle: Sposobnost odbijanja ometanja: Ključna vještina u modernom društvu
sections.screenshot_description: Benjamin Crockett i Otac Kenny Yang raspravljaju o sposobnosti odbijanja ometanja.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett i otac Kenny Yang slažu se da je **sposobnost odbijanja ometanja** najvažnija vještina u modernom svijetu. Benjamin ističe da društvo nije dovoljno raspravljalo o cijeni tehnologije, poput pametnih telefona i društvenih mreža. Iako ti alati omogućuju horizontalnu razmjenu informacija, cijena je gubitak sposobnosti za pravi razgovor, slušanje i fokusiranje. Otac Kenny dodaje da se ova vještina uči kod kuće, ali roditelji često sami daju loš primjer, koristeći telefone tijekom obroka. Primjećuje trend povratka 'glupim telefonima' kao odgovor na preopterećenost pametnim telefonima. Spominje i bratovštinu svećenika u Italiji koja tijekom formacije zabranjuje pametne telefone, a kasnije dopušta samo 'glupe telefone', osim ako je pametni telefon nužan za posao. Zaključuju da ljudi nisu naivni i da će s vremenom prepoznati što je dobro, a što nije.

magisterium.assessment: Usklađeno s upozorenjima Crkve o digitalnoj kulturi koja testira našu pažnju i sposobnost fokusiranja.
magisterium.enrichment: Bez tišine i prostora za duboko razmišljanje, riskiramo gubitak kognitivnih kapaciteta i dubine ljudskih i božanskih interakcija. Kontemplativni pristup i vještina slušanja postaju proročki čin u kulturi brzine i neposrednosti.

citations.document_title: Towards Full Presence: A Pastoral Reflection on Engagement with Social Media
Towards Full Presence: A Pastoral Reflection on Engagement with Social Media

magisterium.assessment_en: Aligned with the Church's warnings about digital culture which tests our attention and ability to focus.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Without silence and space for deep reflection, we risk the loss of cognitive capacities and the depth of human and divine interactions. A contemplative approach and the skill of listening become a prophetic act in a culture of speed and immediacy.

sections.subtitle_en: The ability to reject distractions: A key skill in modern society
sections.screenshot_description_en: Benjamin Crockett and Father Kenny Yang discuss the ability to reject distractions.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett and Father Kenny Yang agree that the **ability to reject distractions** is the most important skill in the modern world. Benjamin points out that society has not sufficiently discussed the price of technology, such as smartphones and social networks. Although these tools enable the horizontal exchange of information, the price is the loss of the ability for real conversation, listening, and focusing. Father Kenny adds that this skill is learned at home, but parents often give a bad example themselves, using phones during meals. He notices a trend of returning to 'dumb phones' as a response to smartphone overload. He also mentions a fraternity of priests in Italija that forbids smartphones during formation, and later allows only 'dumb phones', unless a smartphone is necessary for work. They conclude that people are not naive and that over time they will recognize what is good and what is not.

sections.subtitle: Uloga Crkve u tehnološkoj tranziciji: Moralna pitanja i korištenje slobodnog vremena
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang govori o ulozi Crkve u moralnim pitanjima tehnološke tranzicije.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett pita oca Kennyja o ulozi Crkve u vođenju kroz tehnološku tranziciju, posebno s obzirom na to da se i papa Franjo fokusira na pitanja umjetne inteligencije. Otac Kenny Yang naglašava da Krist i učenja Crkve ostaju nepromijenjeni, dok se situacije u kojima živimo mijenjaju. Crkva ima značajnu ulogu u pratnji ljudi kroz ove promjene. Pozivajući se na Matthewa Sandersa, izvršnog direktora Longbearda, otac Kenny ističe pitanje **korištenja slobodnog vremena**. Kako AI napreduje, imat ćemo više slobodnog vremena, a ključno je pitanje kako ćemo ga iskoristiti. Hoćemo li se prepustiti grijehu i ovisnostima, ili ćemo ga koristiti za pomoć drugima, produbljivanje vjere ili provođenje vremena s obitelji? Ovo je **moralno područje** u kojem Crkva ima mnogo toga za reći, ne ulazeći u tehničke detalje AI-ja, već fokusirajući se na ljudsku osobu i njezinu formaciju.

magisterium.assessment: Savršeno odražava ulogu Crkve u pružanju moralnog vodstva i antropološke vizije usred tehnoloških promjena.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva nudi viziju ljudske osobe kako bi osigurala da duhovne vrijednosti ne postanu podređene materijalnom dobitku. Papa Leo XIV. naglašava da ljudska inteligencija, savjest i sloboda moraju voditi tehničke inovacije.

citations.document_title: Message to the General Assembly of the United Nations Organization (August 22, 1980)
Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Leo XIV Magnifica Humanitas (15 May 2026)

magisterium.assessment_en: It perfectly reflects the role of the Church in providing moral guidance and an anthropological vision amidst technological changes.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church offers a vision of the human person in order to ensure that spiritual values do not become subordinate to material gain. Pope Leo XIV emphasizes that human intelligence, conscience, and freedom must lead technical innovations.

sections.subtitle_en: The role of the Church in the technological transition: Moral questions and the use of free time
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang speaks about the role of the Church in moral issues of technological transition.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett asks Father Kenny about the role of the Church in leading through the technological transition, especially considering that Pope Franjo also focuses on issues of artificial intelligence. Father Kenny Yang emphasizes that Christ and the teachings of the Church remain unchanged, while the situations in which we live change. The Church has a significant role in accompanying people through these changes. Referring to Matthew Sanders, the executive director of Longbeard, Father Kenny highlights the question of **using free time**. As AI advances, we will have more free time, and the key question is how we will use it. Will we surrender to sin and addictions, or will we use it to help others, deepen faith, or spend time with family? This is a **moral area** in which the Church has much to say, not entering into the technical details of AI, but focusing on the human person and their formation.

sections.subtitle: Evolucija obrazovanja u eri AI: Formacija, vrline i vrednovanje sadržaja
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang govori o evoluciji obrazovanja i važnosti vrednovanja sadržaja u eri AI-ja.
sections.content: Razmišljajući o evoluciji obrazovanja, otac Kenny Yang predviđa da će se budućnost fokusirati na **formaciju, moralne vrline te filozofsko i teološko razumijevanje**. Uz to, bit će važne i tehničke vještine. Obrazovanje će se baviti tehnologijom, a ključno će biti educirati studente kako da je koriste. U eri AI-ja, gdje je stvaranje sadržaja postalo lakše, naglasak će se prebaciti s generiranja sadržaja na **vrednovanje sadržaja**. S obzirom na 'buku' i 'lažne vijesti' (deepfakes), sposobnost kritičkog procjenjivanja informacija, posebno onih generiranih umjetnom inteligencijom, postat će temeljna vještina. To će zahtijevati od ljudi da razviju sposobnost razlikovanja autentičnog od neautentičnog, istinitog od lažnog.

magisterium.assessment: Potpuno usklađeno s pozivom na integralni odgoj koji nadilazi puko prenošenje znanja i fokusira se na formaciju osobe.
magisterium.enrichment: Čovjek je pozvan biti suradnik u stvaranju, a ne pasivni potrošač sadržaja generiranog tehnologijom. Obrazovanje mora promicati ljudsko dostojanstvo i osposobiti osobu za odgovorno vršenje slobode i razlučivanje istine.

citations.document_title: To Participants in the Conference "Artificial Intelligence and Care for Our Common Home" (5 December 2025)
Lay Catholics in Schools: Witnesses to Faith
citations.document_author: The Sacred Congregation for Catholic Education

magisterium.assessment_en: Completely aligned with the call to integral education which transcends the mere transmission of knowledge and focuses on the formation of the person.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Man is called to be a collaborator in creation, and not a passive consumer of content generated by technology. Education must promote human dignity and enable the person for the responsible exercise of freedom and the discernment of truth.

sections.subtitle_en: Evolution of education in the AI era: Formation, virtues and evaluation of content
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang speaks about the evolution of education and the importance of evaluating content in the era of AI.
sections.content_en: Reflecting on the evolution of education, Father Kenny Yang predicts that the future will focus on **formation, moral virtues, and philosophical and theological understanding**. In addition, technical skills will also be important. Education will deal with technology, and it will be crucial to educate students on how to use it. In the era of AI, where content creation has become easier, the emphasis will shift from content generation to **content evaluation**. Given the 'noise' and 'fake news' (deepfakes), the ability to critically evaluate information, especially that generated by artificial intelligence, will become a fundamental skill. This will require people to develop the ability to distinguish the authentic from the inauthentic, the true from the false.

sections.subtitle: Kršćanska nada i mreža dobrih ljudi: Optimizam za budućnost
sections.screenshot_description: Otac Kenny Yang izražava optimizam za budućnost kroz kršćansku nadu i zajedništvo.
sections.content: Benjamin Crockett izražava zabrinutost zbog pesimizma mladih studenata koji se boje da će im AI preuzeti poslove, pitajući oca Kennyja je li optimističan glede budućnosti. Otac Kenny Yang odgovara da je **optimističan i nadahnut** prvenstveno zbog svoje kršćanske vjere i nade u Božju pomoć. Osim toga, vjeruje u postojanje **dobrih ljudi**, katolika i nekatolika, diljem svijeta koji teže boljem smjeru. Međutim, naglašava da ti dobri ljudi često nisu povezani. Stoga je ključno **izgraditi mrežu dobrih ljudi** koji će zajedno raditi na rješavanju izazova koje donosi AI i druge prijetnje civilizaciji. Za kršćane, to je i prilika za svjedočenje vjere kroz suradnju s drugima za zajedničko dobro. Otac Kenny zaključuje da, unatoč tome što nije uvijek optimističan u vezi s mnogim stvarima, ima mnogo posla pred sobom i vjeruje da će Bog uvijek pomoći, što je temelj njegove nade.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak izvrsno artikulira kršćansku nadu kao odgovor na suvremene tjeskobe, naglašavajući pouzdanje u Božju providnost i suradnju s ljudima dobre volje. Ovakav pristup izravno odražava poziv Crkve na 'globalizaciju nade' u kontekstu tehnoloških promjena.
magisterium.enrichment: Kršćanska nada nije puki optimizam, već teološka krepost kojom se oslanjamo na milost Duha Svetoga, a ne samo na vlastite snage. Crkva uči da moralna uvjerenja koja proizlaze iz vjere predstavljaju točku susreta sa svim ljudima dobre volje u izgradnji boljeg svijeta. U doba umjetne inteligencije, obrazovanje ima misiju promicanja 'bratskog humanizma' koji tehnološki napredak usmjerava prema općem dobru.

citations.document_title: Catechism of the Catholic Church
Compendium of the Social Doctrine of the Church
citations.document_author: Pontifical Council for Justice and Peace

citations.document_title: Educating to Fraternal Humanism
Educating to Fraternal Humanism

magisterium.assessment_en: The passage excellently articulates Christian hope as a response to contemporary anxieties, emphasizing trust in God's providence and cooperation with people of good will. Such an approach directly reflects the call of the Church to the 'globalization of hope' in the context of technological changes.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Christian hope is not mere optimism, but a theological virtue by which we rely on the grace of the Holy Spirit, and not only on our own strengths. The Church teaches that moral convictions which arise from faith represent a point of encounter with all people of good will in the building of a better world. In the age of artificial intelligence, education has the mission of promoting 'fraternal humanism' which directs technological progress toward the common good.

sections.subtitle_en: Christian hope and a network of good people: Optimism for the future
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Kenny Yang expresses optimism for the future through Christian hope and fellowship.
sections.content_en: Benjamin Crockett expresses concern because of the pessimism of young students who fear that AI will take over their jobs, asking Father Kenny if he is optimistic regarding the future. Father Kenny Yang responds that he is **optimistic and inspired** primarily because of his Christian faith and hope in God's help. Besides that, he believes in the existence of **good people**, Catholics and non-Catholics, throughout the world who strive for a better direction. However, he emphasizes that these good people are often not connected. Therefore it is crucial to **build a network of good people** who will work together on solving the challenges that AI and other threats to civilization bring. For Christians, that is also an opportunity for witnessing the faith through cooperation with others for the common good. Father Kenny concludes that, despite the fact that he is not always optimistic in connection with many things, he has much work before him and believes that God will always help, which is the foundation of his hope.

iterations.theme_en: Education, technology and the human element in a world that is changing
